seoTitle: Fentonbury vs Fern Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fentonbury and Fern Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fentonbury vs Fern Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fentonbury and Fern Tree.
heading: Fentonbury vs Fern Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fentonbury, Tasmania and Fern Tree, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fentonbury has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Fern Tree (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fentonbury or Fern Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fentonbury has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Fern Tree scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fentonbury scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Fern Tree scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fentonbury or Fern Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fentonbury has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
